Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:02 ET
Index puts surge while single-names see call demand despite negative gamma regime
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedge — index puts (1.52) vs single-name calls (0.87) with low correlation at 0.19 creating textbook long stock/short index setup.
  • 2. Negative $6.1B SPY gamma amplifies all moves while XLF sees huge bullish positioning despite sector technical weakness.
  • 3. CPI in 3 days creates urgent positioning window as backwardation in MRVL, TSLA, XLE signals elevated near-term event risk.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma bearish

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices (1.52 put-heavy) and single names (0.87 call-heavy), coinciding with implied correlation at just 0.19 — the lowest stock-specific regime reading in months. This combination creates a textbook dispersion environment where institutions are hedging broad exposure while taking targeted single-name risk, explaining why names like PLUG (+97%) and AEHR (+94%) see massive call flow despite bearish sentiment at -18. Dealer positioning amplifies every signal through negative $6.1B SPY gamma, yet the most compelling block flow runs counter to this regime. XLF's 40,000 June 53 calls at $0.70 represents a $2.8M bullish bet on financials breaking above current resistance, even as the sector shows technical deterioration with JPM in death cross pattern. The size and tenor (June expiry) suggests positioning ahead of potential rate clarity from next week's CPI print, with the trade requiring XLF above $53.70 to profit. Volatility structure confirms near-term event risk as backwardation emerges in MRVL, TSLA, and XLE — all three pricing elevated fear into immediate expiries relative to longer-dated options. Combined with vol divergence flags across 11 names showing rich vol/call heavy patterns, this suggests institutions are paying premium for downside protection while still expressing bullish single-name conviction through dispersion trades.

Layer 1: Sentiment -18Layer 2: GEX -$6.1BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.52/0.87Layer 4: IC 0.19 stock-specific

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
